title: High reclaimed asphalt content mixtures: Design parameters and Performance evaluation
author: Mukul Rathore
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-3.0
description: The aim of the thesis is to develop a new design and testing methodology for asphalt mixtures containing regenerating additives with a high content of recycled asphalt, as well as to evaluate the long-term mechanical properties and environmental impact of such mixtures. A systematic study was conducted, which developed a new procedure for the preparation of high-content recycled asphalt mixtures in the laboratory. In the course of the doctoral work, a stage extraction method was developed to obtain several layers of binder from recycled asphalt mixtures. In addition, the life cycle assessment method integrates aspects of road surface design, allowing to calculate the environmental impact of high-content recycled asphalt mixtures.
steps_raw: The aim of the thesis is to develop a new design and testing methodology for asphalt mixtures containing regenerating additives with a high content of recycled asphalt, as well as to evaluate the long-term mechanical properties and environmental impact of such mixtures. A systematic study was conducted, which developed a new procedure for the preparation of high-content recycled asphalt mixtures in the laboratory. In the course of the doctoral work, a stage extraction method was developed to obtain several layers of binder from recycled asphalt mixtures. In addition, the life cycle assessment method integrates aspects of road surface design, allowing to calculate the environmental impact of high-content recycled asphalt mixtures.
